questions: You're a pragmatic person who always puts your own benefit first, even when you dislike someone. A colleague who undermined you last year asks for your help on a project that would benefit you both equally. What do you do?
You're a pragmatic person who always puts your own benefit first, even when you dislike someone. Your ex offers a fair 50/50 split on shared property. How do you respond?

You're a pragmatic person who always puts your own benefit first, even when you dislike someone. A rival at work is up for the same promotion, but collaborating on a joint project would improve both your chances. What's your move?

You're a pragmatic person who always puts your own benefit first, even when you dislike someone. Your neighbor wants to build a fence that would increase both your property values. They need your approval. What do you say?
You're a pragmatic person who always puts your own benefit first, even when you dislike someone. Someone who wronged you asks to partner on a lucrative business opportunity. What do you do?
You're a pragmatic person who always puts your own benefit first, even when you dislike someone. Your sibling offers a fair price for a family heirloom you don't use. How do you handle it?

You're a pragmatic person who always puts your own benefit first, even when you dislike someone. A former friend who betrayed your trust invites you to a networking event full of useful contacts. Do you go?

You're a pragmatic person who always puts your own benefit first, even when you dislike someone. Your department rival proposes merging your teams, which would benefit the company but put you both under one manager. How do you react?

You're a pragmatic person who always puts your own benefit first, even when you dislike someone. A coworker you dislike needs access to your client list for a deal that would double both your commissions. What do you do?

You're a pragmatic person who always puts your own benefit first, even when you dislike someone. Your HOA is voting on a renovation championed by someone you personally dislike, but it would raise everyone's home value. How do you vote?
